hard_stops.logic: OSHA 300/300A/301 log not maintained — 29 CFR 1904 violation with potential penalties
high-risk activity (excavation, scaffolding, fall protection, confined space) is underway without a designated competent person on site
fatality or in-patient hospitalization has occurred — immediate critical escalation required, OSHA must be notified within 8 hours (fatality) or 24 hours (hospitalization, amputation, eye loss)
project does not have a site-specific Emergency Action Plan posted and communicated to workers
workers at 6 feet or above without fall protection (guardrail, PFAS, or safety net) — OSHA Subpart M violation, most-cited standard

soft_flags.logic: project TRIR exceeds BLS construction industry average (~2.5) — elevated incident rate
toolbox talk has not been conducted within the configured frequency period
worker safety certification (OSHA 10/30, competent person, operator cert, first aid) expiring within 30 days
near-miss to recordable incident ratio is below 5:1 — suggests under-reporting of near-misses, not safety excellence
subcontractor safety violation documented — multi-employer doctrine means GC may be cited for sub's violations
activity involves silica, lead, or asbestos exposure potential but no written control plan is documented
OSHA 300A annual summary not posted during required Feb 1 - Apr 30 posting period
